title: Two Sum
description:
Given an array of integers `nums` and an integer `target`, return indices of the two numbers such that they add up to `target`.

You may assume that each input has exactly one solution, and you may not use the same element twice.

Return the answer in any order.

hint_plan.level_1: Think about what you need to look up quickly when you see a number.
hint_plan.level_2: A hash map can store value → index as you scan the array once.
hint_plan.level_3: For each x, check whether (target - x) was seen before; if yes, return both indices.
hint_plan.level_4: Initialize an empty dict, iterate with index i; for each nums[i], compute complement = target - nums[i]; if complement in dict, return [dict[complement], i]; else store nums[i] -> i.

canonical_solution_summary: Use a hash map from value to index while iterating; for each element, check if complement exists.
